title: [VPlan] Optimize FindLast of (binop %IV, live-in) by sinking.
author: fhahn
description:
When we are finding the last occurrence of a value of an expression that depends on an induction, we can vectorize this by just selecting the IV and sinking the expression in the middle block

This follows one of @ayalz's suggestions during earlier discussions for adding support for CAS/FindLast patterns.

This patch starts with the simplest case, where the selected value is a simple binary expression of a wide IV and a loop-invariant operand.

This should always be profitable, as the current restriction to binary operators ensures that the width of the wide IV matches the original reduction width, we won't introduce any new, wider reduction phi recipes, and remove the boolean reduction + the horizontal reduction in the loop.

I will also share follow-ups to support sinking loads soon.

tests.test_body:
define i64 @findlast_mul_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = mul i64 %iv, 3
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @findlast_non_canonical_iv_with_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 10, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = add i64 %iv, 100
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 2
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @findlast_shl_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = shl i64 %iv, 2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @findlast_sdiv_iv_as_divisor(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = sdiv i64 100, %iv
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @findlast_or_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = or i64 %iv, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

@table = external constant [13 x i16], align 1

define i16 @select_decreasing_induction_icmp_table_i16(i16 noundef %val) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i16 [ 12, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %spec.select, %loop ]
  %gep.table.iv = getelementptr inbounds [13 x i16], ptr @table, i16 0, i16 %iv
  %ld.table = load i16, ptr %gep.table.iv, align 1
  %cmp.table.val = icmp ugt i16 %ld.table, %val
  %iv.next = add nsw i16 %iv, -1
  %spec.select = select i1 %cmp.table.val, i16 %iv.next, i16 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i16 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %spec.select.lcssa = phi i16 [ %spec.select, %loop ]
  ret i16 %spec.select.lcssa
}

define i64 @select_decreasing_induction_icmp_non_const_start(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %rdx.start, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop ], [ %n, %entry ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %cond, %loop ], [ %rdx.start, %entry ]
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, -1
  %gep.a.iv = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.a = load i64, ptr %gep.a.iv, align 8
  %gep.b.iv = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %b, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.b = load i64, ptr %gep.b.iv, align 8
  %cmp.a.b = icmp sgt i64 %ld.a, %ld.b
  %cond = select i1 %cmp.a.b, i64 %iv.next, i64 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp ugt i64 %iv, 1
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %cond
}

@table = external constant [13 x i16], align 1

define i16 @select_decreasing_induction_icmp_table_half(half noundef %val) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i16 [ 12, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %spec.select, %loop ]
  %gep.table.iv = getelementptr inbounds [13 x i16], ptr @table, i16 0, i16 %iv
  %ld.table = load half, ptr %gep.table.iv, align 1
  %cmp.table.val = fcmp ugt half %ld.table, %val
  %iv.next = add nsw i16 %iv, -1
  %spec.select = select i1 %cmp.table.val, i16 %iv.next, i16 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i16 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %spec.select.lcssa = phi i16 [ %spec.select, %loop ]
  ret i16 %spec.select.lcssa
}

define i64 @select_decreasing_induction_icmp_iv_just_within_bounds(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %rdx.start) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %rdx.start, %entry ], [ %cond, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, -1
  %gep.a.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.a = load i8, ptr %gep.a.iv, align 1
  %gep.b.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.b = load i8, ptr %gep.b.iv, align 1
  %cmp.a.b = icmp sgt i8 %ld.a, %ld.b
  %cond = select i1 %cmp.a.b, i64 %iv.next, i64 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %cond
}

define i64 @select_icmp_xor_not_iv(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp.guard = icmp sgt i64 %n, 0
  br i1 %cmp.guard, label %for.body.preheader, label %exit

for.body.preheader:                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %for.body.preheader
  %iv = phi i64 [ %inc, %for.body ], [ 0, %for.body.preheader ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %cond, %for.body ], [ 0, %for.body.preheader ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %0 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %0, 0
  %not.iv = xor i64 %iv, -1
  %cond = select i1 %cmp2, i64 %not.iv, i64 %rdx
  %inc = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %inc, %n
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %exit.loopexit, label %for.body

exit.loopexit:                                    ; preds = %for.body
  %cond.lcssa = phi i64 [ %cond, %for.body ]
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %exit.loopexit, %entry
  %result = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %cond.lcssa, %exit.loopexit ]
  ret i64 %result
}

comments.author: cardigan1008
comments.body:
Hi, I ran this test case with opt (`-passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-interleave=1 -force-vector-width=4`) built on this PR:

```llvm
@a = global [10 x i64] [i64 0, i64 42, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0]

define i64 @findlast_shl_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = sdiv i64 100, %iv
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop

done:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i64 @findlast_shl_expr(ptr @a, i64 10)
  ret i64 %r
}
```

and got the transformed ir:

```llvm
; ModuleID = 'src.ll'
source_filename = "src.ll"

@a = global [10 x i64] [i64 0, i64 42, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0, i64 0]

define i64 @findlast_shl_expr(ptr %a, i64 %n) {
entry:
  %0 = add i64 %n, -1
  %min.iters.check = icmp ult i64 %0, 4
  br i1 %min.iters.check, label %scalar.ph, label %vector.ph

vector.ph:                                        ; preds = %entry
  %n.mod.vf = urem i64 %0, 4
  %n.vec = sub i64 %0, %n.mod.vf
  %1 = add i64 1, %n.vec
  br label %vector.body

vector.body:                                      ; preds = %vector.body, %vector.ph
  %index = phi i64 [ 0, %vector.ph ], [ %index.next, %vector.body ]
  %vec.ind = phi <4 x i64> [ <i64 1, i64 2, i64 3, i64 4>, %vector.ph ], [ %vec.ind.next, %vector.body ]
  %vec.phi = phi <4 x i64> [ splat (i64 -9223372036854775808), %vector.ph ], [ %5, %vector.body ]
  %offset.idx = add i64 1, %index
  %2 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %offset.idx
  %wide.load = load <4 x i64>, ptr %2, align 8
  %3 = icmp eq <4 x i64> %wide.load, splat (i64 42)
  %4 = sdiv <4 x i64> splat (i64 100), %vec.ind
  %5 = select <4 x i1> %3, <4 x i64> %4, <4 x i64> %vec.phi
  %index.next = add nuw i64 %index, 4
  %vec.ind.next = add nuw nsw <4 x i64> %vec.ind, splat (i64 4)
  %6 = icmp eq i64 %index.next, %n.vec
  br i1 %6, label %middle.block, label %vector.body, !llvm.loop !0

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %vector.body
  %7 = select <4 x i1> %3, <4 x i64> %vec.ind, <4 x i64> %vec.phi
  %8 = call i64 @llvm.vector.reduce.smax.v4i64(<4 x i64> %7)
  %9 = icmp ne i64 %8, -9223372036854775808
  %10 = sdiv i64 100, %8
  %11 = select i1 %9, i64 %10, i64 -1
  %cmp.n = icmp eq i64 %0, %n.vec
  br i1 %cmp.n, label %done, label %scalar.ph

scalar.ph:                                        ; preds = %entry, %middle.block
  %bc.resume.val = phi i64 [ %1, %middle.block ], [ 1, %entry ]
  %bc.merge.rdx = phi i64 [ %11, %middle.block ], [ -1, %entry ]
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %scalar.ph, %loop
  %iv = phi i64 [ %bc.resume.val, %scalar.ph ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %bc.merge.rdx, %scalar.ph ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %load = load i64, ptr %gep, align 8
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %load, 42
  %expr = sdiv i64 100, %iv
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %expr, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %done, label %loop, !llvm.loop !3

done:                                             ; preds = %middle.block, %loop
  %sel.lcssa = phi i64 [ %sel, %loop ], [ %11, %middle.block ]
  ret i64 %sel.lcssa
}

define i64 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i64 @findlast_shl_expr(ptr @a, i64 10)
  ret i64 %r
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i64 @llvm.vector.reduce.smax.v4i64(<4 x i64>) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1, !2}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.isvectorized", i32 1}
!2 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.runtime.disable"}
!3 = distinct !{!3, !2, !1}
```

Then ran lli on both cases:

```sh
$ lli src.ll 
$ echo $?
100
$ lli tgt.ll 
$ echo $?
1
```

But this case is verified to be correct with alive2: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Q6UX6f. If this is not a bug, please let me know and I’ll adjust. 

> Note: This is a review assisted with a self-built agent using gemini-3.1-pro-preview. The reproducer was validated manually. Please let me know if anything is wrong. 

In the vectorized form, `%vec.phi` carries the expression value (`%4 = sdiv splat(100), %vec.ind`). However, the middle block mixes IV lanes and expression lanes: `%7 = select %3, %vec.ind, %vec.phi`, followed by `reduce_smax(%7)` and then `sdiv 100, %8`. Here `%3` is the mask from the *last* `vector.body` iteration; in this test the last iteration compares a[5..8] (no 42), so `%3` is all-false and `%7` becomes `%vec.phi` (which still contains the previously-carried expr value 100). The smax reduction therefore yields 100, and the final division computes 100/100 = 1.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: @cardigan1008 thanks for checking, should be fixed in the latest version
